title: Context compaction for long-running subagents
source: Search description: Anthropic 'Effective context engineering for AI agents' — compaction/summarization to stay under window limits (live fetch blocked)
description: Long delegate runs accumulate tool output and file dumps until they hit context limits, degrading reasoning quality near the end of a ticket. Progressive summarization (compact older turns into a running summary, keep recent turns verbatim) keeps the working window dense and on-task, which is the single biggest quality lever for multi-step agents.
proposed_changes: In session.py add a `compact_history(messages, keep_last_n)` helper that, when estimated tokens exceed a threshold, replaces older messages with a single summary message produced by a cheap `claude -p` summarization call. Call it from delegate.py's turn loop before each new model invocation. Persist the summary so it survives resume, and emit a roro 'context_compacted' event with before/after token counts.
